description: Wrong seat rescue scenarios: mismatch, cross-pool move, SMB translations, false discipline, failed test.

examples.context: Tier-1 support hire late and flat on password resets. Fast on complex escalations when pulled in unofficially.
examples.test_assignment: Own one tier-3 escalation through resolution with customer update
examples.resolution: Top performer within weeks. Wrong seat, not lazy.

examples.context: Print shop: sharp pro slow on order entry, fast on complex proof fixes
examples.test_assignment: Rush multi-panel proof with client revision loop
examples.resolution: Moved off data entry. On-time proof turnaround improved.

examples.context: Route filler only restocking snacks, fixes Dex export scripts on break
examples.test_assignment: Diagnose MDB error on problem machine with log capture
examples.resolution: Kept some route work, added technical block. Lateness stopped.

examples.context: Counter hire flat on easy and hard shifts, avoids accountability
examples.test_assignment: Own opening checklist plus one complaint call
examples.resolution: Documented coaching then exit. Not a wrong-seat case.

examples.context: Office admin bored on filing, wanted client project work, test project missed deadline
examples.test_assignment: Coordinate small install with vendor callbacks
examples.resolution: Honest fit talk. Moved to part-time filing with clean reference.

examples.context: Barista who codes bored on register, rebuilt broken scheduling sheet unofficially
examples.test_assignment: Fix vendor order spreadsheet and train one peer
examples.resolution: Still some counter shifts. Energy returned.

examples.context: Another lead asks you to take their failing tech and fix behavior
examples.resolution: Intake only. Observation scheduled. No write-up agreed yet.
Metrics green. Peer pull high. Celebrate.
